page: Microsoft Azure B4ls_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Bsv2 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The server features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable, and it does not include any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data reveals moderate single-core performance alongside weaker multi-core capabilities, which is typical for burstable hardware. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core score both rank in the average tier, while multi-core metrics from stress-ng and Geekbench fall into the weak category. Memory performance is a notable bottleneck, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the poor tier and memory bandwidth read-only tests registering as weak. However, the instance maintains average performance in specific workloads, such as static web serving throughput, gzip compression, and text generation using a small 135M LLM model.

The B4ls_v2 offers a budget-friendly hardware profile with a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:2. The primary tradeoff is its burstable CPU allocation and low memory bandwidth, which limits its suitability for sustained, resource-intensive operations. Multi-threaded decompression and database operations perform poorly under test conditions. Consequently, this server is best suited for low-traffic web servers, light development environments, small-scale background tasks, or basic text processing workloads that experience intermittent CPU utilization spikes rather than continuous heavy loads.
description: Microsoft Azure B4ls_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Bsv2 family, featuring 4 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate average single-core CPU performance, but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities, with Passmark memory scores ranking in the poor tier. The server demonstrates average throughput in static web serving and single-threaded compression tasks. Given its burstable CPU allocation and low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, the B4ls_v2 is a cost-effective option for workloads with variable CPU demands, such as low-traffic web servers, light background processing, and basic text processing tasks.
